title: Fleischmannia viscidipes
taxonomy: Fleischmannia viscidipes is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a member of the tribe Eupatorieae, which consists of plants native to tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Fleischmannia is a group of flowering plants that are primarily found in Central and South America. As a member of this genus, Fleischmannia viscidipes shares characteristics and similarities with other plants within this group. The classification of Fleischmannia viscidipes provides insight into its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants in the Asteraceae family.

intro: Fleischmannia viscidipes, also known as Fleischmannia, is a plant species belonging to the aster family (Asteraceae), native to Central and South America. Traditionally, the plant is used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory issues, due to its reported antipyretic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Fleischmannia viscidipes include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. Fleischmannia is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are commonly consumed to alleviate respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs.
